Context Access (bpy.context)
Â¶
The context members available depend on the area of Blender which is currently being accessed.
Note that all context values are read-only,
but may be modified through the data API or by running operators.

bpy

.

fs
.

(

x

,

heading: bpy.ops.anim.
text:
bpy.ops.anim.
channels_bake
(
*
,
use_scene_range
=
True
,
range
=
(0,
0)
,

1.0
,

=
False

=
'BEZIER'
,

=
True
)
Â¶
Create keyframes following the current shape of F-Curves of selected channels
Parameters
:
use_scene_range
(
bool
) â Use Scene Range, If enabled, the scene start and end frame will be used to determine the bake range (optional)
range
(
Sequence
[
int
]
) â Frame Range, The custom range in which to create new keys. Only used when not using the scene range (array of 2 items, in [-inf, inf], optional)
step
(
float
) â Frame Step, At which interval to add keys (in [0.01, inf], optional)
remove_outside_range
(
bool
) â Remove Outside Range, Removes keys outside the given range, leaving only the newly baked (optional)
interpolation_type
(
Literal
[
'BEZIER'
,
'LIN'
,
'CONST'
]
) â
Interpolation Type, Choose the interpolation type with which new keys will be added (optional)
BEZIER
BÃ©zier â New keys will be BÃ©zier.
LIN
Linear

heading: bpy.ops.anim.

'CONST'
]
) â
Interpolation Type, Choose the interpolation type with which new keys will be added (optional)
BEZIER
BÃ©zier â New keys will be BÃ©zier.
LIN
Linear â New keys will be linear.
CONST
Constant â New keys will be constant.
bake_modifiers
(
bool
) â Bake Modifiers, Bake Modifiers into keyframes and delete them after (optional)

Â¶
The order of selected vertices/edges/faces is modified, based on a given method
Parameters
:
type
(
Literal
[
'VIEW_ZAXIS'
,
'VIEW_XAXIS'
,
'CURSOR_DISTANCE'
,
'MATERIAL'
,
'SELECTED'
,
'RANDOMIZE'
,
'REVERSE'
]
) â
Type, Type of reordering operation to apply (optional)
VIEW_ZAXIS
View Z Axis â Sort selected elements from farthest to nearest one in current view.
VIEW_XAXIS
View X Axis â Sort selected elements from left to right one in current view.
CURSOR_DISTANCE
Cursor Distance â Sort selected elements from nearest to farthest from 3D cursor.
MATERIAL
Material â Sort selected faces from smallest to greatest material index.
SELECTED
Selected â Move all selected elements in first places, preserving their relative order.
Warning: This will affect unselected elementsâ indices as well.
RANDOMIZE
Randomize â Randomize order of selected elements.
REVERSE
Reverse â Reverse current order of selected elements.

set
[

,

) â Create Data, Add data layers on destination meshes if needed (optional)

,

'INVERT_UNSELECTED'

ALL
All â Apply action to all vertex groups.
SELECTED
Selected â Apply to selected vertex groups.
UNSELECTED
Unselected â Apply to unselected vertex groups.
INVERT_UNSELECTED
Invert Unselected â Apply the opposite of Lock/Unlock to unselected vertex groups.

heading: bpy.ops.pose.
text:
bpy.ops.pose.
propagate
(
*
,
mode
=
'NEXT_KEY'
,
end_frame
=
250.0
)
Â¶
Copy selected aspects of the current pose to subsequent poses already keyframed
Parameters
:
mode
(
Literal
[
'NEXT_KEY'
,
'LAST_KEY'
,
'BEFORE_FRAME'
,
'BEFORE_END'
,
'SELECTED_KEYS'
,
'SELECTED_MARKERS'
]
) â
Terminate Mode, Method used to determine when to stop propagating pose to keyframes (optional)
NEXT_KEY
To Next Keyframe â Propagate pose to first keyframe following the current frame only.
LAST_KEY
To Last Keyframe â Propagate pose to the last keyframe only (i.e. making action cyclic).
BEFORE_FRAME
Before Frame â Propagate pose to all keyframes between current frame and âFrameâ property.
BEFORE_END
Before Last Keyframe â Propagate pose to all keyframes from current frame until no more are found.
SELECTED_KEYS
On Selected Keyframes â Propagate pose to all selected keyframes.
SELECTED_MARKERS
On Selected Markers â Propagate pose to all keyframes occurring on frames with Scene Markers after the current frame.
end_frame
(
float
) â End Frame, Frame to stop propagating frames to (for âBefore Frameâ

heading: bpy.ops.pose.

â Propagate pose to all keyframes occurring on frames with Scene Markers after the current frame.
end_frame

float
) â End Frame, Frame to stop propagating frames to (for âBefore Frameâ mode) (in [1.17549e-38, inf], optional)

:
Result of the operator call.

Â¶

EQUIRECTANGULAR
Equirectangular â Spherical camera for environment maps, also known as Lat Long panorama.
EQUIANGULAR_CUBEMAP_FACE
Equiangular Cubemap Face â Single face of an equiangular cubemap.
MIRRORBALL
Mirror Ball â Mirror ball mapping for environment maps.
FISHEYE_EQUIDISTANT
Fisheye Equidistant â Ideal for fulldomes, ignore the sensor dimensions.
FISHEYE_EQUISOLID
Fisheye Equisolid â Similar to most fisheye modern lens, takes sensor dimensions into consideration.
FISHEYE_LENS_POLYNOMIAL

FISHEYE_LENS_POLYNOMIAL
Fisheye Lens Polynomial â Defines the lens projection as polynomial to allow real world camera lenses to be mimicked.
CENTRAL_CYLINDRICAL
Central Cylindrical â Projection onto a virtual cylinder from its center, similar as a rotating panoramic camera.

Â¶
Material data-block to define the appearance of geometric objects for rendering
alpha_threshold
Â¶
A pixel is rendered only if its alpha value is above this threshold (in [0, 1], default 0.5)

blend_method
Â¶
Blend Mode for Transparent Faces (Deprecated: use âsurface_render_methodâ) (default
'OPAQUE'
)
OPAQUE
Opaque â Render surface without transparency.
CLIP
Alpha Clip â Use the alpha threshold to clip the visibility (binary visibility).
HASHED
Alpha Hashed â Use noise to dither the binary visibility (works well with multi-samples).
BLEND
Alpha Blend â Render polygon transparent, depending on alpha channel of the texture.
Type
:

Â¶
Controls the blending and the compatibility with certain features (default
'DITHERED'
)
DITHERED
Dithered â Allows for grayscale hashed transparency, and compatible with render passes and raytracing. Also known as deferred rendering..
BLENDED
Blended â Allows for colored transparency, but incompatible with render passes and raytracing. Also known as forward rendering..

ACCUR

heading: mathutils.Color
text:
Determines which inner part of the mesh will produce volumetric effect (default
'FAST'
)
FAST
Fast â Each face is considered as a medium interface. Gives correct results for manifold geometry that contains no inner parts..
ACCURATE
Accurate â Faces are considered as medium interface only when they have different consecutive facing. Gives correct results as long as the max ray depth is not exceeded. Have significant memory overhead compared to the fast method..
Type
:
Literal[âFASTâ, âACCURATEâ]

Â¶
This object gives access to Colors in Blender.
Most colors returned by Blender APIs are in scene linear color space, as defined by the OpenColorIO configuration. The notable exception is user interface theming colors, which are in sRGB color space.
